title: ESP32 Footstep Power Generator Web App

requirements.text: Create a backend actor that stores lifetime persistent data: total footsteps, total energy generated (Wh), and total runtime (seconds). Expose query and update methods to read and increment these values.

quotes: Data must remain saved after app restart

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores totalFootsteps, totalEnergyGenerated, totalRuntime as stable variables
Provides getLifetimeStats query method returning all three values
Provides updateLifetimeStats update method that increments each value
Data persists across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Build a WiFi connectivity service in the frontend that polls a configurable ESP32 IP address (default: http://192.168.4.1/data) via HTTP GET every 1 second. Parse the JSON response containing steps, power, battery, totalEnergy, usedEnergy, voltage, and current fields. Implement auto-reconnect logic with exponential backoff on failure. Track connection state as ONLINE or OFFLINE.

quotes: Fetch data every 1 second using HTTP GET

acceptanceCriteria: App polls the configured URL every 1 second when ONLINE
IP address is configurable by the user and persisted in localStorage
Connection status updates to OFFLINE within 2 seconds of failed requests
Auto-retry attempts reconnection automatically after failure
Last used IP address is remembered across page reloads

requirements.text: Build a Dashboard screen that displays: footstep count, current power in Watts, battery percentage with charging animation, voltage, current, total energy generated, total energy used, and a prominent ONLINE/OFFLINE system status indicator. Animate a foot icon whenever a new step is detected. Include a power flow animation when power > 0.

quotes: Animate foot icon when step detected

acceptanceCriteria: All 7 data fields (steps, power, battery, voltage, current, totalEnergy, usedEnergy) are displayed
ONLINE/OFFLINE badge updates in real time
Foot icon triggers a CSS animation when the steps value increases
Battery icon shows a pulsing/charging animation when battery < 100% and power > 0
Power flow animation (e.g., flowing dots or wave) is visible when current power > 0
Shows 'Device Offline' message when ESP32 is unreachable

requirements.text: Build a Graph screen with four live-updating line charts: Power vs Time, Steps vs Time, Battery vs Time, and Energy vs Time. Each chart maintains a rolling window of the last 60 data points. Use a charting library (Recharts or Chart.js via react-chartjs-2) suitable for React.
acceptanceCriteria: Four separate charts are displayed on the Graph screen
Each chart updates in real time as new data arrives
Charts show at most 60 data points and scroll/drop old points
Charts are clearly labeled with axis titles and units
Charts are readable on mobile screen widths

requirements.text: Persist lifetime stats (total footsteps, total energy generated, total runtime) in localStorage as a fallback for offline use, and sync increments to the backend canister when connected. On app load, merge localStorage values with backend values taking the maximum of each field.

quotes: Data must remain saved after app restart

acceptanceCriteria: Lifetime stats are written to localStorage on every update
On app start, localStorage data is loaded immediately before backend data arrives
Backend is updated periodically (every 30 seconds) with accumulated deltas
Data survives full page reload

requirements.text: Apply a dark futuristic UI theme with a near-black background, gradient accents in blue (#00BFFF), cyan (#00FFFF), and green (#39FF14), large bold numbers for key metrics, a glowing/neon card style, and a bottom navigation bar for switching between Dashboard and Graph screens. Ensure the layout is fully mobile-optimized.
acceptanceCriteria: Background is dark (#0a0a0f or similar near-black)
Metric cards use neon/glow border effects in blue, cyan, or green
Key numbers (power, steps, battery) are displayed in large bold fonts (≥2rem)
Navigation between Dashboard and Graph is smooth with CSS transitions
Layout is usable on 375px wide mobile screens without horizontal scroll
Bottom navigation bar with Dashboard and Graph tabs is present

requirements.text: Add a Settings panel accessible from the header where the user can view and change the ESP32 IP address/URL. The new URL should be saved to localStorage and take effect immediately on the next poll cycle.

quotes: Auto connect to last used IP address

acceptanceCriteria: Settings panel shows the current ESP32 URL
User can edit and save a new URL
Saved URL persists in localStorage across reloads
Polling uses the updated URL immediately after saving

constraints: Do not use WebSockets; use HTTP polling only
ESP32 communication must originate from the browser (frontend), not the backend canister
All Motoko logic must remain in a single main actor file
nonGoals: Android APK or native mobile app generation
CORS proxy or server-side relay for ESP32 requests
User authentication or Internet Identity integration

required: Futuristic glowing foot/footstep icon on dark background with cyan and green neon glow effect (foot-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Animated-style lightning bolt power icon with blue and cyan gradient neon glow on dark background (power-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Futuristic battery icon with green gradient fill and neon glow outline on dark background (battery-icon.dim_128x128.png)
